name: Toronto

spec.method_detail: Stir with ice until well-chilled. Strain into chilled coupe.

history: From Robert Vermeire's 'Cocktails: How to Mix Them' (1922). The Toronto puts Fernet-Branca in a starring role alongside whisky, which was unusual. It's the cocktail that probably introduced many bartenders to the idea of using amaro as a modifier in small amounts to add complexity. Sometimes called the 'bartender's handshake' variant when Fernet is served as a shot.
